summary: Stellar Estimated Prophet > Space > Take You Home stretch, tight jams
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Estimated Prophet → Space → I Will Take You Home sequence and a spectacular drum battle featuring guest percussion. Jerry’s soaring leads and Brent’s swirling organ textures shine throughout, while the first set delivers solid classics like Mississippi and Queen Jane.
review: February 11, 1989 at the Great Western LA Forum captured one of the Dead’s most exhilarating winter shows. The first set rolled steady with familiar favorites—Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo, New Minglewood Blues, Candyman, Big River and a heartfelt Queen Jane Approximately—that set a warm tone for the night. The real fireworks ignited in the second set: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider flowed into a jaw‑dropping Estimated Prophet → Space → I Will Take You Home stretch that had Jerry soaring on his lead lines while Brent painted lush organ swirls. The drum segment, bolstered by Airto’s guest percussion and the chanting duo Flora/Diana, was called “the most spectacular thing I’d ever seen and heard,” and it propelled the band into a space‑filled groove that culminated in an inspired Eyes Of The World and a fiery Black Peter. The night closed with a rousing Turn On Your Lovelight and a beautifully tender Box of Rain encore, making this Forum show a must‑listen for anyone craving late‑’80s Dead magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are overwhelmingly positive, praising the Estimated Prophet/Space/Take You Home sequence, guest percussion, and overall band tightness; only one review is mixed with no outright negative comments, yielding a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard capture that fully showcases the monster Estimated Prophet → Space → Take You Home sequence and spectacular drum segment.

key_highlights: Monster Estimated Prophet > Space > I Will Take You Home stretch with soaring drums.
Guest percussionists Airto and chanting duo Flora/Diana add a wild, ritualistic dimension to the drum battle.
First set delivers solid classics—Mississippi Half‑Step, Queen Jane Approximately—and tight ensemble playing.
Box of Rain encore provides a heartfelt close after an electrifying second set.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring lead lines throughout, especially on the Estimated Prophet/Space jam; a brief dip noted during Eyes Of The World but overall strong.
band_performance.Phil: Nice all‑around work with a solid bass foundation that kept the band locked in, particularly on the newer material.
band_performance.Bob: Delivered laid‑back, jazzy vocal and guitar phrasing, highlighted on the post‑drums Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Brent: Provided lush organ textures and a lullaby‑like feel on I Will Take You Home; his playing helped lift the second‑set energy.
band_performance.Mickey: Part of the spectacular drum segment, adding power and interplay with guest percussion.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey delivered an awe‑inspiring drum battle, listening closely to each other and embracing the wild percussive additions.
